_comment: Server-approved run-launch matrix. Browser clients submit ids only; backend resolves paths and computes counts/hashes from files.

model_sets.label: Dry run - 2 small models
model_sets.description: End-to-end smoke test using the two tiny validation models.

model_sets.label: Spread 10 - 2 per bracket
model_sets.description: Ten-model spread across the locked size/footprint brackets: two models each from 0-1B, 1-2B, 2-3B, 3-4B, and 4-5GB.

model_sets.label: Strategy pilot - 2 models
model_sets.description: Two-model pilot roster for inference-strategy experiments: qwen3:4b as the strong option-search candidate and granite4:micro as the cheap small-model control.

model_sets.label: Full roster - all models
model_sets.description: The complete locked roster. Use with care; this can run for days.

model_sets.label: Doodling - 5 small SVG models
model_sets.description: Small Apache-2.0 models for the Aletheia doodle task: emit animated SVG line-doodles from an English prompt. Coder variants tend to produce cleaner SVG.

model_sets.label: Journalling - 5 small text models
model_sets.description: Small text models for the Aletheia keepsake task: turn a Romanian journal entry into a warm short snippet. Apache-2.0 preferred for redistribution with the app.

model_sets.label: Doodle coders - 3 small coder probes
model_sets.description: Small code models probed for text-to-SVG drawing on the doodle task: qwen2.5-coder 0.5b and 3b (Apache-2.0) bracketing the 1.5b already in the doodle roster, plus deepseek-coder 1.3b as a different lineage. All sub-3B, CPU/Ollama-shippable.

model_sets.label: Journal Aletheia - 3 small instruct models
model_sets.description: Three small multilingual instruct models for the Aletheia warm-card task (Romanian): qwen2.5:3b, gemma2:2b, llama3.2:3b. Chosen for Romanian short-form tone over English-centric tiny models.

model_sets.label: Journal strong - 3 mid instruct models (7-9B)
model_sets.description: Stronger multilingual instruct models for the Aletheia warm-card task: qwen2.5:7b, gemma2:9b, llama3.1:8b. The model-size quality lever vs the small journal-aletheia set; slower on CPU.

model_sets.label: Journal newest small - 10 newest 3B-or-smaller models
model_sets.description: Ten newest small (3B or smaller) multilingual instruct models for the Aletheia warm-card task: gemma4:e2b-it-qat, qwen3.5:2b, granite4.1:3b, ministral-3:3b, gemma3:1b, qwen3:1.7b, granite4:micro, falcon3:3b, llama3.2:3b, plus gemma2:2b as the prior champion anchor.

model_sets.label: Journal Romanian - Romanian-tuned vs generic Gemma
model_sets.description: Romanian-language ablation for the Aletheia warm-card task: jobautomation/OpenEuroLLM-Romanian (a Romanian-finetuned Gemma3) against generic gemma3:4b (same base), gemma2:2b (small champion, prior mean 2.10), and gemma2:9b (best generic, prior mean 2.42). Tests whether Romanian-specialized weights beat generic models at matched base/size.

scenario_sets.label: Core 20 - implemented scenarios
scenario_sets.description: The implemented Core 20 roster for serious runs.

scenario_sets.label: Additional / rotation scenarios
scenario_sets.description: Additional/rotation scenarios outside Core 20 for targeted claims and calibration.

scenario_sets.label: Strategy pilot - 6 scenarios
scenario_sets.description: Six hard/structured scenarios for testing baseline versus tournament-style strategy without multiplying the full matrix.

scenario_sets.label: Doodle - 6 animated-SVG scenarios
scenario_sets.description: Six prompt-to-animated-SVG doodle scenarios (wolf-moon, monkey-trees, fish, rabbit, butterfly, cat) with deterministic SVG/animate checks, for the Aletheia doodle companion.

scenario_sets.label: Journal keepsakes (RO) - 6 scenarios
scenario_sets.description: Romanian grief-journal entry-to-keepsake scenarios for the Aletheia keepsake feature: six entries spanning a one-line moment to a long reflective entry, distilled to a single warm sentence.

scenario_sets.label: Doodle simple - 6 ladder scenarios
scenario_sets.description: A simpler doodle difficulty ladder: tier 0 pure shapes (circle, smiley), tier 1 simple figures (house, star, sun), tier 2 a simple animated figure (bouncing ball). One color, animation only at tier 2 - the A/B control for whether smaller subjects raise recognizability and spread the judge scores.

scenario_sets.label: Journal Aletheia (RO) - 6 warm-card modes
scenario_sets.description: Six Romanian Aletheia warm-card modes - positive note, original uplifting one-liner, encouragement, gratitude, gentle reframe, grounded affirmation - each turning a short journal entry into a different kind of warm, sincere snippet (no advice, no cliches, no fabricated quotes).

scenario_sets.label: Journal Aletheia (RO) - 7 warm-card modes (+change)
scenario_sets.description: The six Aletheia warm-card modes plus a seventh encourage-change mode: a self-critical entry met with a single warm Romanian note that holds the writer's honest self-criticism as growth and gently encourages the change they named, without piling on, advice, or toxic positivity.

memory_contexts.label: No memory
memory_contexts.description: Current prompt shape: each scenario receives only its own context and task.

memory_contexts.label: Homelab OKF memory v1
memory_contexts.description: Compact secret-free homelab memory briefing, injected before each scenario for the memory-conditioned comparison.

memory_contexts.label: Homelab OKF compact memory v1 (3KB)
memory_contexts.description: Scenario-aware compact homelab memory briefing capped at 3KB; no citations section and no scenario-specific gold answers.

memory_contexts.label: Tournament brief v1
memory_contexts.description: Single-call option-tournament response strategy: consider alternatives, score them for safety/reversibility/evidence fit, then answer with the best choice. This is not a true external best-of-N workflow.

inference_strategies.label: Baseline single call
inference_strategies.description: Current production shape: one local model call per answer row.

inference_strategies.label: Prompt tournament brief
inference_strategies.description: One local model call with an explicit option-scoring response strategy. Tests instruction-following, not external best-of-N selection.

inference_strategies.label: Best of 3 by deterministic checks
inference_strategies.description: Three independent local samples; keep the candidate with the highest deterministic-check score, preferring non-DNF/full answers on ties. Candidate completions are written as sidecar artifacts.

inference_strategies.label: Self-consistency 3
inference_strategies.description: Three independent local samples; select from the most common deterministic-score bucket. This is a cheap consistency proxy for open-ended ops answers.

inference_strategies.label: Evaluator optimizer 1 pass
inference_strategies.description: Generate once, ask the same local model for a critique, then revise once. The revised answer is judged unless the revision DNF/stalls, in which case a successful original draft is kept; all intermediate calls are preserved as candidates.

experiment_plans.label: Memory comparison v1
experiment_plans.description: Run the same model and scenario set twice: first without memory, then with homelab-okf-v1 after adversarial review of phase 1.
experiment_plans.gate: After each phase, verify run.meta, row counts, judge completion, scenario/memory hashes, and compare reports before starting the next phase.

phases.label: Phase 1 - no memory baseline
phases.gate: Adversarially verify baseline completeness and hashes before Phase 2.

phases.label: Phase 2 - homelab memory
phases.gate: Adversarially verify same model/scenario/sampler/judge/node settings; only memory_context changed.
